64_1_1.NKJV: The Elder, To the beloved Gaius, whom I love in truth:
64_1_1.KJV: The elder<S>4245</S> unto the wellbeloved<S>27</S> Gaius<S>1050</S>, whom<S>3739</S> I<S>1473</S> love<S>25</S> in<S>1722</S> the truth<S>225</S>. <sup>in: or, truly</sup>
64_1_1.ESV: The elder to the beloved Gaius, whom I love in truth.
64_1_1.NASB: The elder to the beloved Gaius, whom I love in truth.
64_1_1.NLT: This letter is from John, the elder. I am writing to Gaius, my dear friend, whom I love in the truth.
64_1_1.WEB: The elder to Gaius the beloved, whom I love in truth.
64_1_1.CSB17: The elder: <sup>ⓓ</sup> To my dear friend Gaius, whom I love in the truth. <sup>ⓔ</sup>

The elder,
To my dear friend Gaius, whom I love in the truth.
64_1_1.NRSVCE: The elder to the beloved Gaius, whom I love in truth.

64_1_2.NKJV: Beloved, I pray that you may prosper in all things and be in health, just as your soul prospers.
64_1_2.KJV: Beloved<S>27</S>, I wish<S>2172</S> above<S>4012</S> all things<S>3956</S> that thou<S>4571</S> mayest prosper<S>2137</S> and<S>2532</S> be in health<S>5198</S>, even as<S>2531</S> thy<S>4675</S> soul<S>5590</S> prospereth<S>2137</S>. <sup>wish: or, pray</sup>
64_1_2.ESV: Beloved, I pray that all may go well with you and that you may be in good health, as it goes well with your soul.
64_1_2.NASB: Beloved, I pray that in all respects you may prosper and be in good health, just as your soul prospers.
64_1_2.NLT: Dear friend, I hope all is well with you and that you are as healthy in body as you are strong in spirit.
64_1_2.WEB: Beloved, I pray that you may prosper in all things and be healthy, even as your soul prospers.
64_1_2.CSB17: Dear friend, I pray that you are prospering in every way and are in good health, <sup>ⓕ</sup> just as your whole life is going well. <sup>[1]</sup>
64_1_2.AMP: Beloved, I pray that in every way you may succeed and prosper and be in good health [physically], just as [I know] your soul prospers [spiritually].
64_1_2.MSG: We're the best of friends, and I pray for good fortune in everything you do, and for your good health—that your everyday affairs prosper, as well as your soul!
64_1_2.NIV: Dear friend, I pray that you may enjoy good health and that all may go well with you, even as your soul is getting along well.
64_1_2.NRSVCE: Beloved, I pray that all may go well with you and that you may be in good health, just as it is well with your soul.

64_1_3.NKJV: For I rejoiced greatly when brethren came and testified of the truth that is in you, just as you walk in the truth.
64_1_3.KJV: For<S>1063</S> I rejoiced<S>5463</S> greatly<S>3029</S>, when the brethren<S>80</S> came<S>2064</S> and<S>2532</S> testified<S>3140</S> of the truth<S>225</S> that is in thee<S>4675</S>, even as<S>2531</S> thou<S>4771</S> walkest<S>4043</S> in<S>1722</S> the truth<S>225</S>.
64_1_3.ESV: For I rejoiced greatly when the brothers  came and testified to your truth, as indeed you are walking in the truth.
64_1_3.NASB: For I was very glad when brethren came and testified to your truth, [that is], how you are walking in truth.
64_1_3.NLT: Some of the traveling teachers recently returned and made me very happy by telling me about your faithfulness and that you are living according to the truth.
64_1_3.WEB: For I rejoiced greatly, when brothers came and testified about your truth, even as you walk in truth.
64_1_3.CSB17: For I was very glad when fellow believers came and testified <sup>ⓖ</sup> to your fidelity to the truth — how you are walking in truth. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
64_1_3.AMP: For I was greatly pleased when [some of the] brothers came [from time to time] and testified to your [faithfulness to the] truth [of the gospel message], that is, how you are walking in truth.
64_1_3.MSG: I was most happy when some friends arrived and brought the news that you persist in following the way of Truth.
64_1_3.NIV: It gave me great joy to have some brothers come and tell about your faithfulness to the truth and how you continue to walk in the truth.
64_1_3.NRSVCE: I was overjoyed when some of the friends arrived and testified to your faithfulness to the truth, namely how you walk in the truth.

64_1_4.NKJV: I have no greater joy than to hear that my children walk in truth.
64_1_4.KJV: I have<S>2192</S> no<S>3756</S> greater<S>3186</S> <S>5130</S> joy<S>5479</S> than to<S>2443</S> hear<S>191</S> that my<S>1699</S> children<S>5043</S> walk<S>4043</S> in<S>1722</S> truth<S>225</S>.
64_1_4.ESV: I have no greater joy than to hear that my children are walking in the truth.
64_1_4.NASB: I have no greater joy than this, to hear of my children walking in the truth.
64_1_4.NLT: I could have no greater joy than to hear that my children are following the truth.
64_1_4.WEB: I have no greater joy than this, to hear about my children walking in truth.
64_1_4.CSB17: I have no greater joy <sup>ⓘ</sup> than this: to hear that my children <sup>ⓙ</sup> are walking in truth.
64_1_4.AMP: I have no greater joy than this, to hear that my [spiritual] children are living [their lives] in the truth.
64_1_4.MSG: Nothing could make me happier than getting reports that my children continue diligently in the way of Truth!
64_1_4.NIV: I have no greater joy than to hear that my children are walking in the truth.
64_1_4.NRSVCE: I have no greater joy than this, to hear that my children are walking in the truth.

64_1_5.NKJV: Beloved, you do faithfully whatever you do for the brethren and for strangers,
64_1_5.KJV: Beloved<S>27</S>, thou doest<S>4160</S> faithfully<S>4103</S> whatsoever<S>3739</S> <S>1437</S> thou doest<S>2038</S> to<S>1519</S> the brethren<S>80</S>, and<S>2532</S> to<S>1519</S> strangers<S>3581</S>;
64_1_5.ESV: Beloved, it is a faithful thing you do in all your efforts for these brothers, strangers as they are,
64_1_5.NASB: Beloved, you are acting faithfully in whatever you accomplish for the brethren, and especially [when they are] strangers;
64_1_5.NLT: Dear friend, you are being faithful to God when you care for the traveling teachers who pass through, even though they are strangers to you.
64_1_5.WEB: Beloved, you do a faithful work in whatever you accomplish for those who are brothers and strangers.
64_1_5.CSB17: Dear friend, you are acting faithfully in whatever you do for the brothers and sisters, especially when they are strangers.
64_1_5.AMP: Beloved, you are acting faithfully in what you are providing for the brothers, and especially when they are strangers;
64_1_5.MSG: Dear friend, when you extend hospitality to Christian brothers and sisters, even when they are strangers, you make the faith visible.
64_1_5.NIV: Dear friend, you are faithful in what you are doing for the brothers, even though they are strangers to you.
64_1_5.NRSVCE: Beloved, you do faithfully whatever you do for the friends, even though they are strangers to you;

64_1_6.NKJV: who have borne witness of your love before the church. If you send them forward on their journey in a manner worthy of God, you will do well,
64_1_6.KJV: Which<S>3739</S> have borne witness<S>3140</S> of thy<S>4675</S> charity<S>26</S> before<S>1799</S> the church<S>1577</S>: whom<S>3739</S> if thou bring forward on their journey<S>4311</S> after<S>516</S> a godly<S>2316</S> sort<S>516</S>, thou shalt do<S>4160</S> well<S>2573</S>: <sup>after: Gr. worthy of God</sup>
64_1_6.ESV: who testified to your love before the church. You will do well to send them on their journey in a manner worthy of God.
64_1_6.NASB: and they have testified to your love before the church. You will do well to send them on their way in a manner worthy of God.
64_1_6.NLT: They have told the church here of your loving friendship. Please continue providing for such teachers in a manner that pleases God.
64_1_6.WEB: They have testified about your love before the assembly. You will do well to send them forward on their journey in a way worthy of God,
64_1_6.CSB17: They have testified to your love before the church. <sup>ⓚ</sup> You will do well to send them on their journey <sup>ⓛ</sup> in a manner worthy of God, <sup>ⓜ</sup>
64_1_6.AMP: and they have testified before the church of your love and friendship. You will do well to [assist them and] send them on their way in a manner worthy of God.
64_1_6.MSG: They've made a full report back to the church here, a message about your love. It's good work you're doing, helping these travelers on their way, hospitality worthy of God himself!
64_1_6.NIV: They have told the church about your love. You will do well to send them on their way in a manner worthy of God.
64_1_6.NRSVCE: they have testified to your love before the church. You will do well to send them on in a manner worthy of God;

64_1_7.NKJV: because they went forth for His name’s sake, taking nothing from the Gentiles.

64_1_7.NASB: For they went out for the sake of the Name, accepting nothing from the Gentiles.
64_1_7.NLT: For they are traveling for the Lord, and they accept nothing from people who are not believers.

64_1_8.NKJV: We therefore ought to receive such, that we may become fellow workers for the truth.
64_1_8.KJV: We<S>2249</S> therefore<S>3767</S> ought<S>3784</S> to receive<S>618</S> such<S>5108</S>, that<S>2443</S> we might be<S>1096</S> fellowhelpers<S>4904</S> to the truth<S>225</S>. <sup>fellowhelpers: or, joint-labourers</sup>
64_1_8.ESV: Therefore we ought to support people like these, that we may be fellow workers for the truth.
64_1_8.NASB: Therefore we ought to support such men, so that we may be fellow workers with the truth.
64_1_8.NLT: So we ourselves should support them so that we can be their partners as they teach the truth.
64_1_8.WEB: We therefore ought to receive such, that we may be fellow workers for the truth.
64_1_8.CSB17: Therefore, we ought to support such people so that we can be coworkers with the truth.
64_1_8.AMP: So we ought to support such people [welcoming them as guests and providing for them], so that we may be fellow workers for the truth [that is, for the gospel message of salvation].
64_1_8.MSG: So they deserve any support we can give them. In providing meals and a bed, we become their companions in spreading the Truth.
64_1_8.NIV: We ought therefore to show hospitality to such men so that we may work together for the truth.
64_1_8.NRSVCE: Therefore we ought to support such people, so that we may become co-workers with the truth.

64_1_9.NKJV: I wrote to the church, but Diotrephes, who loves to have the preeminence among them, does not receive us.
64_1_9.KJV: I wrote<S>1125</S> unto the church<S>1577</S>: but<S>235</S> Diotrephes<S>1361</S>, who<S>3588</S> loveth to have the preeminence<S>5383</S> among them<S>846</S>, receiveth<S>1926</S> us<S>2248</S> not<S>3756</S>.
64_1_9.ESV: I have written something to the church, but Diotrephes, who likes to put himself first, does not acknowledge our authority.
64_1_9.NASB: I wrote something to the church; but Diotrephes, who loves to be first among them, does not accept what we say.
64_1_9.NLT: I wrote to the church about this, but Diotrephes, who loves to be the leader, refuses to have anything to do with us.
64_1_9.WEB: I wrote to the assembly, but Diotrephes, who loves to be first among them, doesn’t accept what we say.
64_1_9.CSB17: I wrote something to the church, but Diotrephes, who loves to have first place among them, <sup>ⓑ</sup> does not receive our authority. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
64_1_9.AMP: I wrote something to the church; but Diotrephes, who loves to put himself first, does not accept what we say and refuses to recognize my authority.
64_1_9.MSG: Earlier I wrote something along this line to the church, but Diotrephes, who loves being in charge, denigrates my counsel.
64_1_9.NIV: I wrote to the church, but Diotrephes, who loves to be first, will have nothing to do with us.
64_1_9.NRSVCE: I have written something to the church; but Diotrephes, who likes to put himself first, does not acknowledge our authority.

64_1_10.NKJV: Therefore, if I come, I will call to mind his deeds which he does, prating against us with malicious words. And not content with that, he himself does not receive the brethren, and forbids those who wish to, putting them out of the church.
64_1_10.KJV: Wherefore<S>1223</S> <S>5124</S>, if<S>1437</S> I come<S>2064</S>, I will remember<S>5279</S> his<S>846</S> deeds<S>2041</S> which<S>3739</S> he doeth<S>4160</S>, prating against<S>5396</S> us<S>2248</S> with malicious<S>4190</S> words<S>3056</S>: and<S>2532</S> not<S>3361</S> content<S>714</S> therewith<S>1909</S> <S>5125</S>, neither<S>3777</S> doth<S>1926</S> he himself<S>846</S> receive<S>1926</S> the brethren<S>80</S>, and<S>2532</S> forbiddeth<S>2967</S> them that would<S>1014</S>, and<S>2532</S> casteth<S>1544</S> them out of<S>1537</S> the church<S>1577</S>.
64_1_10.ESV: So if I come, I will bring up what he is doing, talking wicked nonsense against us. And not content with that, he refuses to welcome the brothers, and also stops those who want to and puts them out of the church.
64_1_10.NASB: For this reason, if I come, I will call attention to his deeds which he does, unjustly accusing us with wicked words; and not satisfied with this, he himself does not receive the brethren, either, and he forbids those who desire [to do so] and puts [them] out of the church.
64_1_10.NLT: When I come, I will report some of the things he is doing and the evil accusations he is making against us. Not only does he refuse to welcome the traveling teachers, he also tells others not to help them. And when they do help, he puts them out of the church.
64_1_10.WEB: Therefore if I come, I will call attention to his deeds which he does, unjustly accusing us with wicked words. Not content with this, neither does he himself receive the brothers, and those who would, he forbids and throws out of the assembly.
64_1_10.CSB17: This is why, if I come, I will remind him of the works he is doing, slandering <sup>ⓓ</sup> us with malicious words. And he is not satisfied with that! He not only refuses to welcome fellow believers, but he even stops those who want to do so and expels them from the church.
64_1_10.AMP: For this reason, if I come, I will call attention to what he is doing, unjustly accusing us with wicked words and unjustified charges. And not satisfied with this, he refuses to receive the [missionary] brothers himself, and also forbids those who want to [welcome them] and puts them out of the church.
64_1_10.MSG: If I come, you can be sure I'll hold him to account for spreading vicious rumors about us. As if that weren't bad enough, he not only refuses hospitality to traveling Christians but tries to stop others from welcoming them. Worse yet, instead of inviting them in he throws them out.
64_1_10.NIV: So if I come, I will call attention to what he is doing, gossiping maliciously about us. Not satisfied with that, he refuses to welcome the brothers. He also stops those who want to do so and puts them out of the church.
64_1_10.NRSVCE: So if I come, I will call attention to what he is doing in spreading false charges against us. And not content with those charges, he refuses to welcome the friends, and even prevents those who want to do so and expels them from the church.

64_1_11.NKJV: Beloved, do not imitate what is evil, but what is good. He who does good is of God, but he who does evil has not seen God.
64_1_11.KJV: Beloved<S>27</S>, follow<S>3401</S> not<S>3361</S> that which is evil<S>2556</S>, but<S>235</S> that which is good<S>18</S>. He that doeth good<S>15</S> is<S>2076</S> of<S>1537</S> God<S>2316</S>: but<S>1161</S> he that doeth evil<S>2554</S> hath<S>3708</S> not<S>3756</S> seen<S>3708</S> God<S>2316</S>.
64_1_11.ESV: Beloved, do not imitate evil but imitate good. Whoever does good is from God; whoever does evil has not seen God.
64_1_11.NASB: Beloved, do not imitate what is evil, but what is good. The one who does good is of God; the one who does evil has not seen God.
64_1_11.NLT: Dear friend, don’t let this bad example influence you. Follow only what is good. Remember that those who do good prove that they are God’s children, and those who do evil prove that they do not know God.
64_1_11.WEB: Beloved, don’t imitate that which is evil, but that which is good. He who does good is of God. He who does evil hasn’t seen God.
64_1_11.CSB17: Dear friend, do not imitate what is evil, <sup>ⓔ</sup> but what is good. The one who does good is of God; <sup>ⓕ</sup> the one who does evil has not seen God. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
64_1_11.AMP: Beloved, do not imitate what is evil, but [imitate] what is good. The one who practices good [exhibiting godly character, moral courage and personal integrity] is of God; the one who practices [or permits or tolerates] evil has not seen God [he has no personal experience with Him and does not know Him at all].
64_1_11.MSG: Friend, don't go along with evil. Model the good. The person who does good does God's work. The person who does evil falsifies God, doesn't know the first thing about God.
64_1_11.NIV: Dear friend, do not imitate what is evil but what is good. Anyone who does what is good is from God. Anyone who does what is evil has not seen God.
64_1_11.NRSVCE: Beloved, do not imitate what is evil but imitate what is good. Whoever does good is from God; whoever does evil has not seen God.

64_1_12.NKJV: Demetrius has a good testimony from all, and from the truth itself. And we also bear witness, and you know that our testimony is true.

64_1_12.NASB: Demetrius has received a [good] testimony from everyone, and from the truth itself; and we add our testimony, and you know that our testimony is true.

64_1_12.AMP: Demetrius has received a good testimony and commendation from everyone—and from the truth [the standard of God's word] itself; and we add our testimony and speak well of him, and you know that our testimony is true.
64_1_12.MSG: Everyone has a good word for Demetrius—the Truth itself stands up for Demetrius! We concur, and you know we don't hand out endorsements lightly.
64_1_12.NIV: Demetrius is well spoken of by everyone—and even by the truth itself. We also speak well of him, and you know that our testimony is true.
64_1_12.NRSVCE: Everyone has testified favorably about Demetrius, and so has the truth itself. We also testify for him, and you know that our testimony is true.

64_1_13.NKJV: I had many things to write, but I do not wish to write to you with pen and ink;
